items.title: Arsenal drop title race points in Brentford draw
items.summary: Arsenal extended their lead at the Premier League to four points after they were held at Brentford on Thursday.

items.title: Barcelona taught 'lesson' by Atlético in Copa rout
items.summary: Barcelona coach Hansi Flick said his side were taught a lesson by Atlético Madrid as they suffered a 4-0 loss at the Metropolitano on Thursday in the first leg of the Copa del Rey semifinal between the two teams.

items.title: FA: Tuchel's new deal not related to Man Utd job
items.summary: Football Association chief executive Mark Bullingham said there are "performance conditions" connected to Thomas Tuchel staying on as England head coach beyond the World Cup.

items.title: Ange: Frank sacking 'fair,' but Spurs lack vision
items.summary: Ange Postecoglou has labelled Thomas Frank's sacking a "fair departure" but sympathised with the Dane for having to work in an "environment of uncertainty."

items.title: Sterling joins Feyenoord after Chelsea exit
items.summary: Raheem Sterling has agreed deal to join Feyenoord until end of season, sources have told ESPN.

items.title: Eigen goal én rood: schlemiel García stipt curieus moment aan tegen Atlético
items.summary: Eric García beleefde een meer dan teleurstellende avond met Barcelona in het heenduel van de halve finale van de Copa del Rey tegen Atlético Madrid. De Spanjaard kreeg een eigen doelpunt op zijn naam én pakte een rode kaart. 'Dat had de wedstrijd veranderd.'

items.title: Atlético Madrid put one foot in Copa del Rey final after first-half blitz stuns Barcelona
items.summary: Semi-final first leg: Atlético Madrid 4-0 Barcelona E Garcia 6og, Griezmann 14, Lookman 33, Alvarez 45+2 You must always have faith, Diego Simeone had said and so it was. A biblical storm blew through the Metropolitano, leaving Barcelona torn to pieces and Atlético Madrid closer to a first Copa del Rey final in 13 years than even he could ever have believed. Forty-five extraordinary minutes gave them a 4-0 lead to take to the second leg at Camp Nou in three weeks’ time. An own goal from Eric García and three more, from Ademola Lookman, Antoine Griezmann and Julián Alvarez before the half-time whistle did the kind of damage that only a miracle can repair now. Barcelona could not even begin to fix it here in a second half in which they had a goal ruled out after seven long minutes waiting on the VAR not bringing any more goals. They then had Eric García sent off late on. It was a wonder they were still standing at all, given what had hit them from the very start. Continue reading...

items.title: Arsenal miss chance to restore six-point lead in Brentford draw
items.summary: Arsenal miss out on the chance to restore their six-point lead at the top of the Premier League in a thrilling 1-1 draw at Brentford.

items.title: Arsenal niet in paniek: 'Staan er nog steeds goed voor'
items.summary: Arsenal-keeper David Raya houdt de moed erin na puntenverlies in de Premier League. Brentford hield de koploper op een gelijkspel in eigen huis .

items.title: Man Utd put one foot into WCL quarter-finals with dominant first-leg win
items.summary: Manchester United put one foot into the Women's Champions League quarter-finals after they earned a comprehensive 3-0 victory away at Atletico Madrid in the first leg of their play-off round tie.

items.title: Arsenal miss chance to go six points clear at top of PL with Brentford draw
items.summary: FREE TO WATCH: Highlights from the Premier League match between Brentford and Arsenal.

items.title: Koploper Arsenal ziet Manchester City naderen in de Premier League
items.summary: De loting van de Nations League met het Nederlands elftal, de afstraffing van Barcelona in de Copa del Rey tegen Atlético Madrid en het puntenverlies van Arsenal in de Premier League. Alles kwam voorbij in de VI Live.

items.title: Keane Lewis-Potter header earns Brentford point to deny leaders Arsenal
items.summary: This was billed as a test of Arsenal’s mettle after Manchester City had picked themselves off the canvas against Liverpool at Anfield to reignite the title race. But having seen their lead at the top whittled down to just three points since the weekend, Mikel Arteta’s side showed their fallibility as they were held by a dogged Brentford side who felt that they should have even claimed victory. Just when they needed to put in a statement performance, Arsenal were edgy throughout and struggled to create anything of note until Noni Madueke opened the scoring. Keane Lewis-Potter’s equaliser that came from a Michael Kayode long throw was fully deserved as Keith Andrews’ side showed exactly why they are enjoying such a successful season since he stepped up to replace Thomas Frank last summer. Continue reading...

items.title: Barça komt horrorhelft niet te boven: Atlético met één been in bekerfinale
items.summary: Barcelona is donderdagavond flink tegen de lamp gelopen in het heenduel van de halve finale van de Copa del Rey. Atlético Madrid besliste de wedstrijd al in de eerste helft door vier goals te maken.

items.title: Super League newcomers York claim shock win over Hull KR in season opener
items.summary: York Knights pulled off the most magical of victories as they got their first season in the Betfred Super League up and running with a 19-18 victory over defending champions Hull KR in the opening match of the campaign.

items.title: Terland and Malard set Manchester United on course for last eight in win over Atlético
items.summary: Manchester United took a big step towards the quarter-finals of the Women’s Champions League by sealing a comfortable lead in the first leg of their playoff against Atlético Madrid after goals from Elisabeth Terland, Melvine Malard and Julia Zigiotti Olme. “I think it was [a professional win],” a delighted Marc Skinner, the United manager, said. “It was ­difficult for both teams on the pitch. I can understand it with the number of storms they had ... but I did think it affected the football. I thought we were ruthless. The three goals were fantastic. I felt like our defending as a whole team was excellent.” Continue reading...

items.title: Price flexes muscles to win Premier League night in Antwerp
items.summary: Gerwyn Price flexed his muscles to defeat Michael van Gerwen and seal his first Premier League nightly win of the season in Antwerp, with Luke Littler and Luke Humphries both sitting outside the top four.

items.title: Arsenal drop more title-race points with draw at Brentford
items.summary: Arsenal missed the chance to re-establish their six-point lead at the top of the Premier League as an impressive Brentford side held them to a 1-1 draw.

items.title: Kampioenbibbers bij Arsenal: puntenverlies bij Brentford door assist Van den Berg
items.summary: Arsenal heeft donderdagavond punten verspeeld in de Premier League. De koploper kwam niet verder dan een gelijkspel op bezoek bij Brentford. Omdat Manchester City eerder in de speelronde wel won, loopt de achtervolger twee punten in.

items.title: Tuchel extended England stay for 'amazing players'
items.summary: Thomas Tuchel says he extended his contract as England manager through to Euro 2028 because of the "amazing group of players" at his disposal.
